name: Two Pointers (converging)
summary: Because the array is sorted, put one pointer at each end: if their sum is too small move left up, too big move right down, and equal is the answer. Each step discards one element for good.
visualizationIntent: init: the sorted array as cells; left pointer at 0, right at the last index. loop: while left < right. update (L6): compute total = numbers[left] + numbers[right]; readout 'a + b = total vs target'. check (L7,9): compare total to target. move (L10/L12): total < target discards the left cell and steps left right; total > target discards the right cell and steps right left — discarded cells dim. return (L8): total == target — both end cells glow result (the answer pair, 1-indexed).

lineExplanations.3: Start a pointer at the smallest value (index 0).
lineExplanations.4: Start a pointer at the largest value (last index).
lineExplanations.5: Keep going while the two pointers haven't met.
lineExplanations.6: Add the two end values to get their current sum.
lineExplanations.7: Check whether that sum equals the target.
lineExplanations.8: It does — return the two indices, converted to 1-based.
lineExplanations.9: Otherwise, check whether the sum is too small.
lineExplanations.10: Sum too small — move the left pointer up to a larger value.
lineExplanations.12: Sum too big — move the right pointer down to a smaller value.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def twoSum(self, numbers, target): — numbers is sorted ascending; target is the sum to reach.
syntaxExplanations.3: left = 0 — index of the smallest value.
syntaxExplanations.4: right = len(numbers) - 1 — index of the largest value.
syntaxExplanations.5: while left < right: — keep going until the pointers meet.
syntaxExplanations.6: total = numbers[left] + numbers[right] — the sum of the current two ends.
syntaxExplanations.7: if total == target: — did we hit the target?
syntaxExplanations.8: return [left + 1, right + 1] — return the two indices, converted to 1-based.
syntaxExplanations.9: elif total < target: — is the sum too small?
syntaxExplanations.10: left += 1 — move left up to a larger value.
syntaxExplanations.11: else: — the remaining case, where the sum was greater than the target.
syntaxExplanations.12: right -= 1 — move right down to a smaller value.
